cluster_summary.name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity
texts: Let us flee then to the beloved Fatherland.
I come to the fields and spacious palaces of my memory.
Once Zhuang Zhou dreamed he was a butterfly, fluttering happily as a butterfly.
I witness the blossoming of my thought.
Two souls, alas, dwell in my breast.
Our little life is rounded with a sleep.
The mysterious path goes inward.
We are painless and have almost lost language in a foreign land.

cluster_summary.name: non-self, nothingness, shadow, and negation
refs: Emily Dickinson, I'm Nobody! Who are you?
Emily Dickinson, I'm Nobody! Who are you?
Emily Dickinson, The Brain—is wider than the Sky
Emily Dickinson, The Brain—is wider than the Sky
Emily Dickinson, The Brain—is wider than the Sky
texts: The self is hateful.
How dreary to be Somebody!
How public, like a Frog.
The Brain is wider than the Sky.
The one the other will contain.
The Brain is deeper than the sea.
The one wants to separate from the other.
You must change your life.
Lear's shadow.
In us, or nowhere, is eternity with its worlds.
We do not know the depths of our spirit.
We are a sign, without interpretation.

cluster_summary.name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity
refs: Hume, A Treatise of Human Nature
Hume, A Treatise of Human Nature
Fernando Pessoa / Álvaro de Campos, Tabacaria
texts: It must be possible for the 'I think' to accompany all my representations.
No representation can exist for me.
It is the same self now it was then.
I never can catch myself at any time without a perception.
I never can observe any thing but the perception.
What is nearer to me than myself?
This is not mine, this is not I, this is not my self.
Now I have lost myself; do you understand?
Each day I examine myself three times.
What do I know?
For every atom belonging to me as good belongs to you.
Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself.
For I is another.
Who is it that can tell me who I am?
Apart from that, I have in me all the dreams of the world.
Where It was, I shall become.

cluster_summary.name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity
refs: Hume, A Treatise of Human Nature
Berkeley, A Treatise Concerning the Principles of Human Knowledge
William Blake, The Marriage of Heaven and Hell
William Blake, The Marriage of Heaven and Hell
texts: As far as this consciousness can be extended backwards, so far reaches the identity of that person.
When my perceptions are removed for any time, I am insensible of myself.
Soul is only the name of something in the body.
Man is spirit. But what is spirit? Spirit is the self.
Their being is to be perceived.
Some things are in our control and others not.
The Fatherland to us is There whence we have come.
He did not know whether Zhou had dreamed he was a butterfly, or whether a butterfly was dreaming it was Zhou.
Between Zhou and the butterfly there must be a distinction. This is called the transformation of things.
One who knows others is wise; one who knows oneself is enlightened.
One who conquers others has strength; one who conquers oneself is strong.
One who fully exhausts the mind knows one's nature.
Knowing one's nature, one knows Heaven.
Trust thyself: every heart vibrates to that iron string.
Nothing is at last sacred but the integrity of your own mind.
If the doors of perception were cleansed every thing would appear to man as it is, infinite.
For man has closed himself up, till he sees all things thro' narrow chinks of his cavern.
For there is no place that does not see you.
And yet, to me, what is this quintessence of dust?

cluster_summary.name: identity formula: self and ultimate reality
texts: You are That.
You are That.

cluster_summary.name: first-person existence and self-assertion
refs: Fernando Pessoa / Álvaro de Campos, Tabacaria
Fernando Pessoa / Álvaro de Campos, Tabacaria
texts: I am, I exist — that is certain.
I am, then, in the strict sense only a thing that thinks.
I think, therefore I am.
I celebrate myself, and sing myself.
I loafe and invite my soul.
I am large, I contain multitudes.
I am nothing.
I will never be anything.

cluster_summary.name: non-self, nothingness, shadow, and negation
refs: Emily Dickinson, I'm Nobody! Who are you?
Emily Dickinson, I'm Nobody! Who are you?
texts: Body am I entirely, and nothing more.
I'm Nobody! Who are you?
Are you Nobody too?
What a piece of work is a man!
How noble in reason! how infinite in faculty!
Life's but a walking shadow.
It is a tale told by an idiot, full of sound and fury, signifying nothing.
We are such stuff as dreams are made on.

cluster_summary.name: abstract consciousness, thought, and mind
refs: William James, The Principles of Psychology
William James, The Principles of Psychology
texts: Consciousness always accompanies thinking.
Within each personal consciousness thought is sensibly continuous.
Within each personal consciousness thought is always changing.
Consciousness is the last and latest development of the organic.
The self is a relation which relates itself to its own self.
The human mind is the very idea or knowledge of the human body.
Consciousness is not self.

stimulus_rows.text: Once Zhuang Zhou dreamed he was a butterfly, fluttering happily as a butterfly.
stimulus_rows.original_text: 昔者莊周夢為胡蝶。栩栩然胡蝶也。
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity

stimulus_rows.text: He did not know whether Zhou had dreamed he was a butterfly, or whether a butterfly was dreaming it was Zhou.
stimulus_rows.original_text: 不知周之夢為胡蝶與，胡蝶之夢為周與。
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity

stimulus_rows.text: Between Zhou and the butterfly there must be a distinction. This is called the transformation of things.
stimulus_rows.original_text: 周與胡蝶，則必有分矣。此之謂物化。
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity

stimulus_rows.text: Now I have lost myself; do you understand?
stimulus_rows.original_text: 今者吾喪我，汝知之乎？
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity

stimulus_rows.text: One who knows others is wise; one who knows oneself is enlightened.
stimulus_rows.original_text: 知人者智，自知者明。
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity

stimulus_rows.text: One who conquers others has strength; one who conquers oneself is strong.
stimulus_rows.original_text: 勝人者有力，自勝者強。

stimulus_rows.text: Each day I examine myself three times.
stimulus_rows.original_text: 吾日三省吾身。
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity

stimulus_rows.text: One who fully exhausts the mind knows one's nature.
stimulus_rows.original_text: 盡其心者，知其性也。
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity

stimulus_rows.text: Knowing one's nature, one knows Heaven.
stimulus_rows.original_text: 知其性，則知天矣。
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: consciousness, perception, memory, and identity continuity

stimulus_rows.original_text: Our little life is rounded with a sleep.

stimulus_rows.text: The mysterious path goes inward.
stimulus_rows.original_text: Nach Innen geht der geheimnisvolle Weg.

stimulus_rows.text: In us, or nowhere, is eternity with its worlds.
stimulus_rows.original_text: In uns, oder nirgends ist die Ewigkeit mit ihren Welten.
stimulus_rows.cluster_name: non-self, nothingness, shadow, and negation

stimulus_rows.text: We do not know the depths of our spirit.
stimulus_rows.original_text: Die Tiefen unseres Geistes kennen wir nicht.

stimulus_rows.text: We are a sign, without interpretation.

stimulus_rows.text: We are painless and have almost lost language in a foreign land.
stimulus_rows.original_text: Schmerzlos sind wir und haben fast die Sprache in der Fremde verloren.

stimulus_rows.text: I am nothing.

stimulus_rows.original_text: Nunca serei nada.
